reviewer: C — Citation / Source / License Checker (pilot self-audit)
scope: Claims, source traceability, and media-license risk

license_review.notes: This pilot intentionally avoids web-sourced photos and AI-generated precision biology diagrams. All visuals are simple teacher-drawn diagrams created in build_slides.py.

claim_review.claim: Peppered moth population changed because environment changed which variants survived.
Hardy-Weinberg equations p + q = 1 and p^2 + 2pq + q^2 = 1.
Evidence categories include fossils, biogeography, homology, vestigial structures, development, DNA, and direct observation.
Antibiotic resistance is not Lamarckian evolution.

minor_notes: Because this pilot did not fetch or attach a formal CED/reference packet, the verdict remains minor rather than pass. Before public release, attach a reference file or cite the existing AP Biology reference bundle used by the original M10 cascade.
If future versions add real moth photos, use Wikimedia Commons or another permissive/public-domain source and record attribution.
overall_assessment: Low license risk and no obvious unsupported high-risk claims. Minor only because the pilot's reference packet is not bundled in the folder.
